# Data Center Stack Tradeoffs

This chapter turns the previous data center topics into decision matrices.

## Chapter Job

Give the reader a practical method for choosing a stack: cloud GPU rental,
managed serverless GPUs, bare metal GPU clusters, specialized inference
providers, or fully owned infrastructure.

## Stack Options

| Option | Strength | Cost or risk |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Managed API | Fastest product integration | Lowest infrastructure control |
| GPU cloud instance | Good for experiments and medium control | Availability and price volatility |
| Managed GPU platform | Faster deployment path | Platform constraints |
| Bare metal cluster | Maximum control and stable utilization | Procurement and operations burden |
| Specialized inference system | Strong fit for specific workload classes | Portability and ecosystem risk |

## Decision Template

Score each option across:

- Time to first benchmark.
- Time to production.
- Hardware control.
- Network control.
- Runtime control.
- Cost predictability.
- Failure isolation.
- Vendor lock-in.
- Team skill required.

## Bridge

After the data center choices are visible, the book moves inward: what is inside
the node, what matters in the accelerator, and which knobs are actually
configurable by an inference engineer.
